---
title: "Bayesian conjugate analysis for federated statistical inference"
subtitle: "CRS Colloqium, 13 October 2025"
author: "Peter Degen"
format: 
    revealjs:
        theme: [custom.scss]
        width: 1920
        height: 1080
        margin: 0.1
        incremental: true 
bibliography:
  - BaySeq.bib
---

## Problem setting: generalized linear model (GLM)

\

$\mathrm{E}[\mathbf{y|\mathbf{X}}] = g^{-1}(\mathbf{X}\boldsymbol{\beta})$

\

We wish to estimate regression coefficients $\boldsymbol{\beta}$ from the data $(\mathbf{X}, \mathbf{y})$.

## Federated inference

:::: {.columns}

::: {.column width="50%"}
![](images/federated_inference.svg){width=85%}
:::

::: {.column width="50%"}

::: {.fragment}
### Motivation
:::

- Sharing of individual patient data (IPD) restricted due to regulatory constraints
- Perform statistical inference using summary statistics

::: {.fragment}
### Practical considerations
:::

- **One-shot:** one round of communication
  - Contrast to federated machine learning: iterative
- **Parallel:** each center communicates independently

::: {.fragment}
### Methods
:::

- Multivariate meta-analysis
- BFI: Bayesian Federated Inference [@jonker_bayesian_2024]
- BCA: Bayesian Conjugate Analysis (Degen and Held)
- Distributed MCMC (not shown)
:::

::::

::: aside
<a href="https://www.flaticon.com/free-stickers/hospital" title="hospital stickers">Hospital stickers created by kerismaker - Flaticon</a>
:::

## Bayesian federated inference (BFI)

- Approximate $\boldsymbol{\beta}$ using second-order Taylor expansion centered around maximum aposteriori estimate

## Bayesian conjugate analysis (BCA)

\

### Normal linear model (not GLM)

\

$\begin{align}
    &\mathrm{Prior:} &p(\boldsymbol{\beta}, \tau | \boldsymbol{\beta}_0, \boldsymbol{\Lambda}_0, a_0, b_0) &= N(\boldsymbol{\beta} | \boldsymbol{\beta}_0, (\tau\boldsymbol{\Lambda}_0)^{-1})\times \operatorname{Gamma}(\tau|a_0, b_0)\\
    &\mathrm{Posterior:} &p(\boldsymbol{\beta}, \tau | \mathbf{X}, \mathbf{y}) &= N(\boldsymbol{\beta} | \boldsymbol{\beta}_n, (\tau\boldsymbol{\Lambda}_n)^{-1})\times \operatorname{Gamma}(\tau|a_n, b_n),
\end{align}$
\
\
where the **Bayesian update formulas** are given by
\
\
$\begin{align}
    \boldsymbol{\beta}_n &= \boldsymbol{\Lambda}_n^{-1}(\boldsymbol{\Lambda}_0\boldsymbol{\beta}_0 + \mathbf{X}^{\top} \mathbf{y}) \\
    \boldsymbol{\Lambda}_n &= \boldsymbol{\Lambda}_0 + \mathbf{X}^{\top}\mathbf{X} \\
    a_n &= a_0 + n/2 \\
    b_n &= b_0 + \frac{1}{2}(\mathbf{y}^{\top}\mathbf{y} + \boldsymbol{\beta}_0^{\top}\boldsymbol{\Lambda}_0\boldsymbol{\beta}_0- \boldsymbol{\beta}_n^{\top}\boldsymbol{\Lambda}_n\boldsymbol{\beta}_n).
\end{align}$

## BCA for federated inference (linear model)

\

Let's use a non-informative *reference prior* for center 1. Then, sequential application of the update formulas over all $L$ centers yields the following one-shot formulas:

$\begin{align}
    \boldsymbol{\beta}_L &= \boldsymbol{\Lambda}_L^{-1} (\sum_{l=1}^{L} \mathbf{X}_l^{\top}\mathbf{y}_l)\\
    \boldsymbol{\Lambda}_L &= \sum_{l=1}^{L} \mathbf{X}_l^{\top}\mathbf{X}_l\\
    a_{L} &= \frac{1}{2}\sum_{l=1}^{L}n_{l} - \frac{m}{2} = \frac{n-m}{2}\\
    b_{L} &= \frac{1}{2}\sum_{l=1}^{L}\mathbf{y}_l^{\top}\mathbf{y}_l - \frac{1}{2}(\boldsymbol{\beta}_L^{\top}\boldsymbol{\Lambda}_L\boldsymbol{\beta}_L)
\end{align}$

## BCA for federated inference (linear model)
\
Maximum aposteriori estimator is equivalent to ordinary least squares on **combined data**:

\begin{equation}
\boldsymbol{\tilde{\beta}}_L = (\sum_{l=1}^{L} \mathbf{X}_l^{\top}\mathbf{X}_l)^{-1}( \sum_{l=1}^{L} \mathbf{X}_l^{\top}\mathbf{y}_l)
= (\mathbf{X}^{\top}\mathbf{X})^{-1}(\mathbf{X}^{\top}\mathbf{y})
= \boldsymbol{\hat{\beta}}_{Combined}
\end{equation}

![](images/collage.params.Nurses_local_int.png){width=80% fig-align="center"}

::: {.fragment}
<div style="
  position: absolute;
  bottom: 80px;
  left: 46%;
  transform: translateX(-50%);
  width: 55px;
  height: 160px;
  border: 3px solid red;
  border-radius: 50%;
  background: none;
"></div>

<div style="text-align: center; color: red">
"Nice agreement" (Jonker et al.)
</div>
:::

## BCA for federated inference (GLM)
\
Maximum aposteriori estimator equivalent to **multivariate fixed-effect meta-analysis**:

\begin{equation}
\boldsymbol{\tilde{\beta}}_L = (\sum_{l=1}^L\boldsymbol{\hat{\Lambda}_l})^{-1}\sum_{l=1}^L\boldsymbol{\hat{\Lambda}_l}\boldsymbol{\hat{\beta}_l}
\end{equation}

![](images/collage.params.trauma_shuffled.png){width=80% fig-align="center"}

## Bibliography
